title: quercus × giffordii
intro: Quercus × giffordii, also known as Gifford's oak, is a hybrid plant species between the Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia) and the Interior Live Oak (Quercus wislizenii). The medicinal properties of Quercus × giffordii lie in its tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Quercus × giffordii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to promote overall well-being.
